compiled_state.title: Add .test-index merge=union to .gitattributes
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Add a union merge driver entry for .test-index in .gitattributes. This ensures that when two worktree branches both append new entries to .test-index, git auto-resolves the merge by keeping both sets of entries (union strategy) instead of producing a conflict marker.

### Files to modify
- .gitattributes (add: /.test-index merge=union)

### Test approach (Given/When/Then)
- Given: two branches that both add different entries to .test-index
- When: one branch is merged into the other
- Then: the resulting .test-index contains entries from both branches with no conflict markers

### Files to create
- tests/hooks/test-gitattributes-union-driver.sh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] .gitattributes contains merge=union entry for .test-index
  Verify: grep -q 'test-index.*merge=union' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.gitattributes
- [ ] tests/hooks/test-gitattributes-union-driver.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/hooks/test-gitattributes-union-driver.sh
- [ ] Union merge test passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/hooks/test-gitattributes-union-driver.sh

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3/6: Tests written ✓ — RED confirmed: test fails with missing merge=union entry and actual merge conflict
